headline: Nothing cancels this year's CMF phone due to RAM prices
summary: Nothing has cancelled its next CMF budget phone for this year. The decision is due to a significant rise in memory (RAM) prices. Co-founder Akis Evangelidis confirmed the delay on X.

headline: NASA selects Eric Schmidt's rocket company for a 2028 mission to Mars
summary: Relativity Space, led by Eric Schmidt, will launch NASA's Aeolus payload to Mars. The mission is scheduled for 2028 under a new public-private partnership. Relativity Space will provide the spacecraft, rocket, and cruise operations.

headline: Hue's wired wall modules bring non-smart lights into its ecosystem
summary: Philips Hue has launched new wired wall modules for smart lighting. These modules integrate existing non-smart lights into the Hue system. Hue also introduced new Play lamps and E14 bulb upgrades.

headline: The NTS Radio Player brings the best of internet radio to your hi-fi
summary: NTS Radio and Atonemo launched a dedicated player for NTS's streaming stations. The player connects to almost any stereo or speaker setup. It also supports major streaming services like AirPlay 2 and Spotify Connect.

headline: George Lucas Cast in Minions & Monsters After Revealing Massive Fandom
summary: Star Wars creator George Lucas has been cast in "Minions & Monsters." His casting follows revelations of his significant fandom for Minions. Lucas has discussed future Minions movie appearances with Illumination.

headline: CD Projekt Red Co-CEO Hopes Witcher 4 Wins Back Players After 'Heartbreaking' Cyberpunk 2077 Launch
summary: CD Projekt Red co-CEO Michał Nowakowski acknowledges "Cyberpunk 2077"'s rough launch. He feels the studio's "redemption arc" is not yet complete. Nowakowski hopes "The Witcher 4" will help win back player trust.

headline: Amazon Walks Away from Nearly Complete Sam Altman Movie Just Months After OpenAI Deal
summary: Amazon has dropped "Artificial," a movie about Sam Altman. The film was reportedly nearly complete when Amazon walked away. This comes months after Amazon struck a deal with OpenAI.

headline: Beat the Summer Heat With This $8 Portable Turbo Fan
summary: A portable turbo fan is available for just $8. It's an affordable option to combat summer heat. The deal is highlighted as a way to "stay frosty."

headline: Driver dies and 33 people seriously injured as Bedford train crash declared major incident
summary: A train crash near Bedford resulted in one fatality and 33 serious injuries. The incident, which occurred on Friday, left nearly 90 people hurt. Authorities have declared the crash a major incident.

headline: PM under pressure from Labour MPs and ministers to set timetable for exit
summary: The Prime Minister faces pressure from Labour MPs and ministers. They are advising Starmer to set out a timetable for his exit. The transport secretary is reportedly among those urging a timeline.

headline: Israel and Hezbollah agree ceasefire, US says, as more Lebanon strikes reported
summary: The US announced a ceasefire agreement between Israel and Hezbollah. This agreement aims to prevent further escalation in the region. More strikes in Lebanon were reported despite the announced deal.

headline: Why candidates dress up and run in major UK elections
summary: Candidates, like Count Binface, often wear costumes in UK elections. Their diverse motivations are part of a longstanding political tradition. This practice highlights unique aspects of British democratic processes.
